dependency_respect.details: All four UoWs respect the dependency chain. UOW-001 (T1) has no predecessors and is correctly in batch 1. UOW-002 (T2) and UOW-003 (T3) both depend on UOW-001 and are both in batch 2, which follows batch 1. UOW-004 (T4) depends on UOW-002 and UOW-003 and is correctly in batch 3. No dependency is violated.

parallelism_safety.details: Batch 2 parallelises UOW-002 (routing/guard infrastructure, pure logic layer) and UOW-003 (dialog UI component, standalone Angular component). The safety rationale provided is accurate: both UoWs only consume the dirty-state signal produced by UOW-001 — neither reads nor writes the other's outputs. No shared files, no shared mutable state, and negligible merge conflict risk. Parallelisation is safe.

risk_ordering.details: Highest-risk UoW (UOW-001: unknown codebase dirty-state patterns, foundational to all others) is correctly placed first. Second-highest-risk UoW (UOW-002: unknown intercept strategy — CanDeactivate vs. click handler) is in batch 2, as early as dependency constraints allow. UOW-003 (moderate risk: existing dialog component unknown) is parallel to UOW-002 in batch 2 — optimal placement. UOW-004 (integration complexity, but risks de-risked by prior batches) is correctly deferred to batch 3. Risk ordering is consistent with the risk_ordered_items metadata.

completeness.details: All four task_ids from tasks.yaml (T1, T2, T3, T4) are scheduled via source_task_id in assignments.json. No task_ids are missing; no extraneous UoWs present.

rationale_quality.details: All three batches have clear, specific batch_rationale fields explaining the composition and ordering logic. Each UoW has its own rationale field. The assignments.json metacognitive_context provides a decision_rationale and lists three alternatives_discarded with concrete reasons. The parallelization_opportunities block adds a specific safety_rationale for the Batch 2 parallel pair. Rationale quality is high throughout.

schedule_analysis.estimated_parallelism_benefit: Batch 2 runs UOW-002 and UOW-003 in parallel, saving one full sequential unit-of-work slot on the critical path for a 3-task, 4-UoW story. Meaningful time reduction for this story size.

notes: The 3-batch sequential-parallel-sequential structure is the canonical optimal schedule for this dependency graph. The critical path (UOW-001 → UOW-002 → UOW-004) is correctly identified. The only tasks not on the critical path (UOW-003) is correctly parallelised with the second critical-path node (UOW-002). No improvements recommended.

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: Performed all programmatic gate scripts first (schema validation and cycle detection), confirmed both exit-0 pass, then proceeded to full rubric evaluation traversing the dependency graph manually against tasks.yaml task_ids. This approach ensures objective gates are not conflated with subjective rubric judgment.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Skip gate scripts and evaluate purely by inspection
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Protocol requires scripts to run as hard programmatic gates; inspection alone is not sufficient and cannot guarantee topological correctness.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Run full rubric even on gate failure
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Protocol specifies skipping full rubric analysis on gate failure to avoid misleading partial scores; gates passed so moot here.

knowledge_gaps: Actual file paths modified by each UoW are not known — parallelism safety assessment relies on the schedule's stated separation of concerns (routing layer vs. dialog component layer), which is plausible but unverified without access to mcs-products-mono-ui.

tool_anomalies.anomaly: Relative path 'WI-5035632/planning/assignments.json' failed (file not found); absolute path from repo root 'agent-context/WI-5035632/planning/assignments.json' succeeded. Scripts must be run from the repo root.
